title: Project Overview and Vision
description: Understand DeepScan's core contribution: a training-free framework for visually grounded reasoning in LVLMs. Learn about the three-stage pipeline (Hierarchical Scanning, Refocusing, Evidence-Enhanced Reasoning) and the CVPR 2026 paper.
languageLesson: Markdown documentation for research project communication and setup instructions

title: System Architecture and Design
description: Explore the overall architecture of DeepScan including the paper-to-code mapping, runtime entry points, service architecture (BLIP, LangSAM, SAM2), and the three-environment design (lavis, langsam, deepscan).
languageLesson: Architectural documentation explaining service-oriented design with HTTP endpoints

title: Environment and Dependency Configuration
description: Learn how DeepScan manages three isolated conda environments with specific dependencies. Each YAML file specifies packages for different expert services and the main LVLM runtime.
languageLesson: YAML configuration for Python environment management with conda

title: Experiment Flow and Batch Evaluation
description: Understand the complete experiment pipeline from shell scripts to batch processing. Learn how run.py handles dataset sharding, async sample creation, policy dispatch, and JSONL output generation.
languageLesson: Detailed documentation of async execution patterns and data parallelism

title: Batch Evaluation Entry Point
description: Explore the main batch runner script that loads question TSVs, instantiates policies dynamically, processes samples asynchronously, and writes predictions. Understand CLI arguments and data sharding logic.
languageLesson: Python async/await patterns, argparse CLI design, and asyncio coroutine orchestration

title: Single-Sample Inference Entry Point
description: Learn the single-sample debugging interface for testing one question at a time. Useful for understanding individual sample flow without batch processing overhead.
languageLesson: Alternative entry point design for testing and debugging

title: Base Policy Abstraction and VLM Interface
description: Understand the QuestionSample base class that defines the policy interface, image preprocessing logic, async LVLM generation, and result formatting. This is the foundation for all reasoning strategies.
languageLesson: Abstract base classes, image processing with PIL, async OpenAI API client usage

title: Core Utilities and Helper Functions
description: Explore shared utilities for dataset loading, data sharding, client management, and common operations used across the pipeline. Understand OpenAI client load balancing across GPUs.
languageLesson: Utility functions, GPU-aware client balancing, data splitting algorithms

title: VLM Runtime Wrapper for Qwen Models
description: Learn how Qwen vision-language models are loaded, configured, and wrapped for inference. Understand GPU device allocation, dtype handling, and multi-image input support.
languageLesson: Transformers library model loading, device management, batch image processing

title: Monte Carlo Tree Search (MCTS) Framework
description: Understand the MCTS algorithm implementation including node definitions, action spaces (repeat_question, zoom_out), reward calculation, and simulation mechanics. This is the core search strategy.
languageLesson: MCTS algorithm, tree node structures, UCB-based action selection with PUCT constant

title: DeepScan-Enhanced MCTS with Grounding
description: Explore the OursMCTSQuestionSample class that implements the complete DeepScan pipeline: hierarchical scanning for cue discovery, evidence justification, refocusing with MCTS, and ensemble evidence-enhanced reasoning.
languageLesson: Policy-specific implementation extending base MCTS with visual grounding

title: Visual Grounding and Heatmap Processing
description: Learn the hierarchical scanning stage: querying BLIP for attention heatmaps, filtering with Otsu thresholding, extracting connected components, and generating candidate evidence regions. Foundation of cue discovery.
languageLesson: Image processing with OpenCV, heatmap filtering, connected component analysis

title: Control Point Extraction and SAM2 Integration
description: Understand how SAM2 point-prompt segmentation is used to convert heatmap centroids into binary masks. Learn polygon point sampling and mask clustering for evidence generation.
languageLesson: Mask processing with NumPy/OpenCV, DBSCAN clustering, binary mask operations

title: HTTP Client for Expert Services
description: Explore how the main runtime communicates with three expert services: BLIP for heatmaps, LangSAM for object detection, and SAM2 for point-prompt segmentation. Understand request/response serialization.
languageLesson: HTTP requests library, base64 image encoding/decoding, numpy array serialization

title: BLIP Search Expert Service
description: Learn the BLIP-ITM based search expert that computes patch-wise Grad-CAM attention maps. This service handles image blocks and returns heatmaps used for hierarchical scanning.
languageLesson: FastAPI service design, LAVIS model integration, Grad-CAM computation

title: LangSAM Visual Expert Service
description: Explore the visual expert service that performs text-conditioned object detection and segmentation using GroundingDINO and LangSAM. Used for localization tasks during MCTS refinement.
languageLesson: Batch processing with async queues, FastAPI request handling, mask serialization

title: SAM2 Point-Prompt Segmentation Service
description: Understand the SAM2 inference service that converts point prompts into binary masks. Handles positive/negative point collections and returns masks used to generate evidence crops.
languageLesson: SAM2 model loading and inference, base64 numpy serialization, point coordinate handling
